title: ageratina lasioneura
intro: Ageratina lasioneura, also known as Mexican mountain mint, is a flowering plant native to Mexico and parts of Central America, belonging to the mint family Lamiaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to treat digestive issues, colds, and fever, due to its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Ageratina lasioneura include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by infusing the leaves in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
